title: Minor Poets of the Caroline Period, Vol. III
summaries: "Minor Poets of the Caroline Period, Vol. III" by George Saintsbury is a historical collection of poetry written in the early 20th century. This volume features works by several poets, including John Cleveland, Thomas Stanley, Henry King, Thomas Flatman, and Nathaniel Whiting, providing insights into the literary contributions of the Caroline period. The collection aims to present a comprehensive and accessible edition of their poems, highlighting the complexities and richness of their styles and themes.  The opening of this volume establishes the editorial journey undertaken by George Saintsbury to compile and present the works of these minor poets. He reflects on the challenges he faced in sourcing the materials, modernizing the texts, and ensuring authenticity amidst previous editions that often included spurious attributions. Saintsbury emphasizes his commitment to making this literary treasure accessible, while also expressing gratitude for the assistance he received along the way. The introduction sets the stage for the selected poets and their contributions, offering a brief glimpse into the historical context and literary merit of Cleveland and his contemporaries.
